LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE APR-23-2019 HEARING ON DEFENDANTS ANDERSON ENTERPRISES INC., VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS ANDERSON ENTERPRISES, INC. AND VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC.'S DEMURRER TO THE COMPLAINT BY PLAINTIFFS DONALD R. MCCAFFREE AND SARA MCCAFFREE IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUDULENT CONCEALMENT AND IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIFTH AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL AND NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION. PLAINTIFFS HAVE 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO CURE THE DEFECT IF THEY CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN SCHULMAN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/EPS)

ORDER ON DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE IMPROPER DAMAGE ALLEGATIONS FROM PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE APR-23-2019 HEARING ON DEFENDANTS ANDERSON ENTERPRISES INC.. VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE, THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS ANDERSON ENTERPRISES INC. AND VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS FROM THE COMPLAINT BY PLAINTIFFS DONALD R. MCCAFFREE AND SARA MCCAFFREE IS DENIED. PLAINTIFFS' CLAIM FOR FRAUDULENT CONCEALMENT IS SUPPORTED BY ALLEGATIONS THAT DEFENDANTS ACTIVELY CONCEALED FROM PURCHASERS THAT A DEFECT IN THE VEHICLE'S ENGINE POSED A SERIOUS SAFETY RISK TO DRIVERS, WHICH IS A SUFFICIENT ALLEGATION OF WILLFUL AND CONSCIOUS DISREGARD OF THE RIGHTS OR SAFETY OF OTHERS TO SUPPORT A CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES PURSUANT TO CIV. CODE SEC. 3294. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN SCHULMAN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/EPS)

Off Calendar Mar-09-2021 Continued To Jun-28-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-26-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Armando Lopez, Esq. of Knight Law Group LLP, 310.552.2250, for the Plaintiffs;
Atty. Shaun Kim, Esq. of Squire Patton Boggs (US) LLP, 213.624.2500, for Defendants.

DEFENDANTS ANDERSON ENTERPRISES INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

The matter is called. ARGUMENT is held. At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendants' motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendants' motion for summary adjudication is granted and denied in part.

The motion for summary adjudication is denied as to causes of action 1-3 for violation of the Song-Beverly Consumer Warranty Act. Defendants failed to maintain their initial burden of production as they fail to show that the 4-year statute of limitations bars the claims. (See Krieger v. Nick Alexander Imports, Inc. (1991) 234 Cal.App.3d 205, 213-215; Cal. Commercial Code sec. 2725(2) ["A cause of action accrues when the breach occurs, regardless of the aggrieved party's lack of knowledge of the breach. A breach of warranty occurs when tender of delivery is made, except that where a warranty explicitly extends to future performance of the goods and discovery of the breach must await the time of such performance the cause of action accrues when the breach is or should have been discovered."].)

(See Word minutes for the Court's complete ruling.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/RBU)

The Court indicates that if the Presiding Judge grants the parties' stipulated request for a trial continuance, the parties may email this department to request that the March 26, 2021 hearing date be continued further out (but should do so as far in advance of the hearing as possible).

Motion To Stay Proceedings Pending Determination Of Petition For Coordination; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar May-28-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS ANDERSON ENTERPRISES INC. AND VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC.'S MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS PENDING DETERMINATION OF PETITION FOR COORDINATION

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED. This action is stayed until a ruling on the petition to coordinate is made by the Los Angeles Superior Court after the hearing on June 6, 2019. This court has the inherent power to issue such a stay. (Jordache Enters., Inc. v. Brobeck, Phleger & Harrison (1998) 18 Cal.4th 739, 758.) Given the nearly 30 other matters involving substantially similar Lemon law issues and allegations of fraud and the voluminous discovery propounded by plaintiffs Donald R. McCaffree and Sara McCaffree, there is good cause to issue an interim stay pending a determination on the petition to coordinate. The stay will promote the interests of justice by avoiding waste of the parties' time and conserving court resources related to discovery. Plaintiffs' concerns about unnecessary delay are speculative and do not outweigh the risk of prejudice to defendants in responding to repetitive discovery. Order signed. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600 (Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com) (302/RBU)

No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS ANDERSON ENTERPRISES INC., VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendants Anderson Enterprises, Inc. and Volkswagen Group of America, Inc.'s demurrer to the complaint by plaintiffs Donald R. McCaffree and Sara McCaffree is overruled as to the fourth cause of action for fraudulent concealment and is sustained with leave to amend as to the fifth and sixth causes of action for intentional and negligent misrepresentation. Plaintiffs have 20 days leave to amend to cure the defect if they can do so in good faith. (see wod minutes for complete details)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)

Defendants Volkswagen Group Of America, Inc.S And Anderson Enterprises, Inc.S Motion To Strike
Off Calendar Apr-23-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS ANDERSON ENTERPRISES INC.. VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC.'S Motion To Strike.

Defendants Anderson Enterprises Inc. and Volkswagen Group of America, Inc.'s motion to strike the punitive damages allegations from the complaint by plaintiffs Donald R. McCaffree and Sara McCaffree is DENIED. Plaintiffs' claim for fraudulent concealment is supported by allegations that Defendants actively concealed from purchasers that a defect in the vehicle's engine posed a serious safety risk to drivers, which is a sufficient allegation of willful and conscious disregard of the rights or safety of others to support a claim for punitive damages pursuant to Civ. Code 3294.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)
